title: Prompt-response latency: over-eager, agent starts before the caller finishes (FAIL latency)

latency_bounds.note: A non-null premature_start_sec means the agent's response onset LED the caller's turn end: the agent stepped on the human. Because the agent's onset precedes the caller's offset there is no clean response gap, so response_gap_sec is null by construction, and premature_start_sec carries the lead.

transcript.agent: Sure, I can look that up for you...
transcript.caller: what time does the pharmacy close on Sunday and are you...

why_it_matters: The caller is still mid-question when the agent barges back in with its answer. The agent's response onset leads the caller's turn end by about half a second, so it talks over the tail of the human's own question. This is the opposite failure to lat-02: not too slow, but too eager. premature_start_sec fires; response_gap_sec is null because there was no silent hand-off to measure.
